title: Post-Award — Subawards
content:
Subawards. A subaward is issued to a subrecipient for assistance in carrying out a specified programmatic effort on a sponsored project. Morgan State University passes through a portion of the sponsored award to the other entity. Subawards may also be called subgrants, subagreements, or subcontracts.

Definition (Subrecipient): A subrecipient is a legal entity to which a subaward is made and which is accountable for the use of the funds provided.

Key rules:
- The prime award terms and conditions apply to all subrecipient entity types.
- Subrecipients bear responsibility for programmatic and administrative decisions and for compliance with the prime award terms.
- Subaward monitoring is coordinated by ORA Post-Award staff in collaboration with the PI.

Resources: A training video, 'Subawards: What You Need to Know,' is available, along with associated forms and policies accessible via the ORA website. Detailed subaward procedures are documented in PI Handbook 3: Grant-Related Processes.

Additional details: The 'Subawards: What You Need to Know' training video is embedded on the page as a YouTube video (https://www.youtube.com/embed/zoDvRuwD0l4) titled 'Zoom video about how subawards are process at Morgan State University.' The page also clarifies that the term 'subaward' includes awards made by a subrecipient to a lower-tier subrecipient when prior approval is obtained.

Contacts:
- Pre-Award subaward questions: Ailing Zhang, MS — ailing.zhang@morgan.edu
- Post-Award subaward questions: Matthew Lee, MBA, PMP — matthew.lee@morgan.edu; Poline Mirithu — poline.mirithu@morgan.edu
- General ORA: 443-885-4044 (phone), 443-885-8280 (fax), ask.ora@morgan.edu, Tyler Hall Suite 304, Baltimore, MD 21251.
